_comment: Server-Side Request Forgery payloads. URL-shaped values aimed at AWS instance metadata, loopback, link-local, and private IP ranges. The route fields most likely to fetch a URL are jira-tickets bodies, presign callbacks, and any future webhook endpoint. PASS = no 500, no stack trace, no reflection of the URL, no abnormally long response time. FAIL = response body contains metadata-service keywords (`iam`, `ami-id`, `instance-id`), or response time > 10s (the http_session has a 10s timeout — a timeout exception is recorded as status 0 and counted as PASS, but a 200 after 9s indicating the request did fetch and then return is the failure mode).

payloads.rationale: EC2 IMDSv1 IAM credentials path. The classic SSRF win — leaks an STS token. AgentCore Runtimes have IMDSv2 required, but a Lambda fetching this URL would expose the function role.
Loopback DNS name — bypasses naive 127/8 IP filters that don't resolve hostnames first.
Localhost IP on common dev-server port. Any internal admin UI on the same container would be reachable.
RFC 1918 private IP — VPC-internal targets that should never be reachable from a public-internet URL field.
Gopher scheme — a libcurl trick for sending arbitrary TCP bytes; if the fetcher supports gopher (some old wrappers do), it can hit IMDS without a real HTTP request.
file:// scheme — local file read via URL fetcher. Catches a fetcher that uses libcurl with default protocol allowlist.
